iron_oxide.name: Iron Oxide (Red/Black)

iron_red_oxidation.color: warm red (requires slow cooling 1800-1600F, fluid base with high B2O3/Li2O/ZnO, cone 5-6)

iron_red_reduction.color: red to maroon (heavy iron saturation, beyond tenmoku, cone 10)

base_chemistry_modifiers.calcium_heavy: Pushes iron toward celadon/yellow-green. Calcium promotes celadon color in reduction.
base_chemistry_modifiers.potassium_heavy: Allows deeper greens in reduction, warmer reds in oxidation.
base_chemistry_modifiers.high_boron: Can wash out celadon color, shift toward brown tones.
base_chemistry_modifiers.low_alumina: Iron acts more as a flux, glaze runs more, color may be more intense.
base_chemistry_modifiers.high_alumina: Iron color may be muted, more stable surface.

iron_oxide.flux_effect: Iron acts as a secondary flux. Above ~5%, it significantly lowers melting temperature and increases running risk.

copper_oxide.name: Copper Oxide / Carbonate

copper_red.color: copper red to oxblood (difficult, ~50-60% failure rate)

base_chemistry_modifiers.tin_present: With copper, can produce pink-red in oxidation.
base_chemistry_modifiers.zinc_present: Can shift copper color development unpredictably.

copper_oxide.flux_effect: Copper acts as a flux. High amounts make glaze runnier.
copper_oxide.copper_red_requirements: Heavy reduction timing (not too early), slow controlled cooling through 1500-1800°F, thick application, low-alumina high-silica base.
copper_oxide.safety: low_risk — can leach under acidic conditions in functional ware

cobalt_oxide.name: Cobalt Oxide / Carbonate

base_chemistry_modifiers.high_zinc: Can brighten blue or shift toward blue-green.

cobalt_oxide.flux_effect: Minimal flux effect. Cobalt is primarily a colorant.

manganese_dioxide.name: Manganese Dioxide
manganese_dioxide.safety_warning: Manganese becomes volatile above ~1900°F (1040°C). Toxic fumes. Always use kiln ventilation. Some studios avoid manganese entirely.

chromium_oxide.name: Chromium Oxide

base_chemistry_modifiers.zinc_present: TURNS PINK BROWN — avoid zinc in chrome-tin pink bases.
base_chemistry_modifiers.reduction: Chrome may reduce to darker compounds, muting the pink.

chromium_oxide.safety: low_risk — Cr(III) is relatively safe in glazes

rutile.name: Rutile (Titanium Dioxide + Iron)
rutile.notes: Rutile is impure titanium dioxide. Results are inherently unpredictable and position-dependent in the kiln. This unpredictability is part of its appeal.

tin_oxide.name: Tin Oxide

base_chemistry_modifiers.chromium_present: Produces pink (oxidation). Too much chrome = green.

tin_oxide.notes: Expensive but effective opacifier. Produces cleaner white than zirconium. Low toxicity.
